nativeRange.habitat: Has been reported over sand and rubble slopes and soft mud bottoms of outer reef environments, generally at depths of about 9–60 m. Adults are commonly observed singly or in pairs, retreating into burrows in soft substrate when disturbed.
nativeRange.biotope: Reef-associated sand and rubble slopes with burrowable substrate

diet.notes: Has been reported to feed on small zooplankton and benthic invertebrates in the wild. In captivity, generally accepts meaty fare such as frozen mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, finely chopped seafood, Cyclops, and similar small frozen items. Newly imported specimens are commonly considered cryptic and finicky; enriched live brine or live blackworms have been reported to help entice reluctant feeders. Generally recommended to feed small portions multiple times per day.

summary: A slender, eel-like Indo-Pacific wormfish striped in metallic blue and orange-yellow; generally peaceful and reef-safe, but a notorious jumper that requires a tightly sealed lid and a deep sand bed for burrowing.
careNotes: The Curious Wormfish (Gunnellichthys curiosus), also widely sold as the Neon Worm Goby, Rainbow Goby, or Yellow-striped Worm Eel, is an elongate microdesmid that hovers just above the substrate by day and dives into burrows in soft sand at the first sign of threat. Generally considered peaceful and reef-safe — it ignores corals and sessile invertebrates and does not bother small ornamental shrimp. Best kept in a mature reef aquarium with a deep, fine sand bed for burrowing, ample rockwork with caves and overhangs for security, gentle to moderate flow, and stable parameters; the species has been reported to be sensitive to poor water quality and shipping/handling stress. **A tightly fitting lid with no gaps is essential** — jumping is by far the most commonly reported cause of captive death (LiveAquaria, Quality Marine, Reeflex all explicitly warn). May be kept singly or as a small group provided all individuals are introduced to the tank simultaneously and have plenty of horizontal swimming space. Tank-size sources disagree widely (LiveAquaria 30 US gal / ~114 L, Reeflings 75 L, Quality Marine 50 US gal / ~190 L, Reeflex 250 L); a 200 L footprint with at least ~90 cm of length is used here, biased above the strict average given the species' active mid/bottom swimming behaviour, ~12 cm adult length, escape risk, and need for both open sand bed and rocky cover. Care difficulty is rated 'advanced' here — sources disagree (LiveAquaria 'difficult', Reeflings 'moderate to easy', Quality Marine 'moderate', Reeflex notes 'no successful captivity reports yet'); the average plus the well-documented cryptic feeding behaviour and stress sensitivity pushes it above intermediate. Feeding difficulty is marked 'difficult' because new arrivals frequently refuse prepared foods initially and have been reported to require live or live-enriched offerings before transitioning to frozen.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species product page reached via the dartfish collection root. Provided care-level ('difficult'), water-parameter, tank-size (30 US gal / ~114 L), temperament, reef-safe, and diet figures, plus the explicit jumping/lid warning.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Microdesmidae, Gobiiformes), max size 12 cm SL, depth range 9–60 m, environmental temperature 22–27°C, and Indo-Pacific range (Seychelles and Mauritius to the Hawaiian and Society islands). IUCN status Least Concern.

additional.name: Reef2Reef wiki
additional.notes: Assigned first secondary returned HTTP 403 Forbidden. Fell back through the slice's secondary list to Reeflex, Reeflings, and Quality Marine.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size 12 cm, temperature 22–27°C, 250 L minimum recommendation (used as high-end anchor in the tank-size disagreement), Indo-Pacific range, and the jumping/lid warning for wormfishes and gobies. Notes 'no successful captivity reports yet' — used as a difficulty signal.

additional.name: Reeflings
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.022–1.025, max size 12 cm, peaceful temperament, reef-safe rating, and solitary social preference. Listed minimum tank as 75 L — used as low-end anchor in the tank-size disagreement.

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked SG 1.020–1.025, temperature 22.2–25.6°C, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, ~12.7 cm max size, peaceful temperament, reef-safe rating, deep-sand-bed and rockwork needs, and explicit tight-lid jumping warning. Listed minimum tank as 50 US gal / ~190 L.
